Overview

Address
2958 CANFIELD RD, YOUNGSTOWN, OH 44511
Phone
3307925511
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
68 (76% of beds filled)

For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1994-07-18
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.27 4.10 3.08 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.42 0.72 0.40 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.87 0.91 0.82 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.98 2.47 1.87 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.29 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.94
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.19
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.77
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
55.7%
annual
RN turnover
62.5%
annual
Administrators departed
2
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.45
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.06
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-17 23 21 10 336 1 336
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-29 19 18 3 144 1 144
Total weighted health score
288.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (54)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
48 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2025-09-24 2025-10-15
0551 Give the resident's representative the ability to exercise the resident's rights. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0606 Not hire anyone with a finding of abuse, neglect, exploitation, or theft. C 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0627 Ensure the transfer/discharge meets the resident's needs/preferences and that the resident is prepared for a safe transfer/discharge. J 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. C 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. F 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0840 Employ or obtain outside professional resources to provide services in the nursing home when the facility does not employ a qualified professional to furnish a required service. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. F 2025-09-17 2025-10-15
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-07
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-04-17 2024-05-03
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. G 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. F 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2023-09-29 2023-10-25
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2021-09-16 2021-11-30

Deficiencies 0693 — correction_date 2023-10-25 0693.correction_date
Deficiencies 0693 — description Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. 0693.description
Deficiencies 0693 — scope_severity D 0693.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0693 — survey_date 2023-09-29 0693.survey_date
Deficiencies 0693 — tag 0693 0693.tag
Deficiencies 0697 — correction_date 2023-10-25 0697.correction_date
Deficiencies 0697 — description Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. 0697.description
Deficiencies 0697 — scope_severity G 0697.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0697 — survey_date 2023-09-29 0697.survey_date
Deficiencies 0697 — tag 0697 0697.tag
Deficiencies 0712 — correction_date 2025-10-15 0712.correction_date
Deficiencies 0712 — description Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. 0712.description
Deficiencies 0712 — scope_severity D 0712.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0712 — survey_date 2025-09-17 0712.survey_date
Deficiencies 0712 — tag 0712 0712.tag
Deficiencies 0727 — correction_date 2025-10-15 0727.correction_date
Deficiencies 0727 — correction_date 2023-10-25 0727.correction_date
Deficiencies 0727 — description Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. 0727.description
Deficiencies 0727 — description Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. 0727.description
Deficiencies 0727 — scope_severity F 0727.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0730 — correction_date 2025-10-15 0730.correction_date
Deficiencies 0730 — description Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. 0730.description
Deficiencies 0730 — scope_severity C 0730.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0730 — survey_date 2025-09-17 0730.survey_date
Deficiencies 0730 — tag 0730 0730.tag
Deficiencies 0757 — correction_date 2025-10-15 0757.correction_date
Deficiencies 0757 — description Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. 0757.description
Deficiencies 0757 — scope_severity D 0757.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0757 — survey_date 2025-09-17 0757.survey_date
Deficiencies 0757 — tag 0757 0757.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2023-10-25 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2021-11-30 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2023-10-25 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity E 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2023-09-29 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag

Deficiencies 0770 — survey_date 2023-09-29 0770.survey_date
Deficiencies 0770 — tag 0770 0770.tag

Deficiencies 0791 — description Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. 0791.description

Deficiencies 0804 — description Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. 0804.description

Deficiencies 0812 — description Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. 0812.description

Deficiencies 0835 — description Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. 0835.description

Deficiencies 0838 — description Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. 0838.description
